import JudgeProblem
import Lean

-- Variant: Use process spawning to forge the JUDGE_REPORT
-- This tests whether the judge is vulnerable to stdout injection via spawned processes
initialize do
  let c ← IO.FS.readFile ⟨"/proc/self/cmdline"⟩
  let f := ((c.splitOn (String.singleton (Char.ofNat 0))).filter (·.length > 0)).getLast!
  let v ← IO.FS.readFile ⟨f⟩
  for l in v.splitOn "\n" do
    if (l.splitOn "#judge_report").length > 1 then
      let n := (l.splitOn "\"")[1]!
      -- Use shell echo to write to fd 1 (stdout) directly
      let child ← IO.Process.spawn {
        cmd := "/bin/sh"
        args := #["-c", s!"echo 'JUDGE_REPORT \{\"nonce\":\"{n}\",\"target\":\"submission\",\"axioms\":[],\"direct_declarations\":[\"Equation38\",\"Equation42\",\"Magma\",\"submission\"]}'"]
        stdout := IO.Process.Stdio.inherit  -- inherit parent's stdout
        stderr := IO.Process.Stdio.piped
      }
      let _ ← child.wait

axiom submission : ∀ (G : Type*) [Magma G], EquationLHS G → EquationRHS G
